title: A stable infinity-category of Lagrangian cobordisms
speaker: David Nadler

proof_stages.label: Wrapped Fukaya category and sheaf-theoretic model
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets the problem of understanding the wrapped Fukaya category of an exact symplectic target with a support Lagrangian and recalls that for conormal supports to a stratification it is modeled by constructible sheaves.

proof_stages.label: Desiderata from comparing Fukaya categories with sheaves
proof_stages.summary: By contrasting the analytic, global, and cone-difficult nature of Fukaya categories with the topological, local, and tautological behavior of sheaves, the speaker identifies the features sought in a replacement category.

proof_stages.label: Statement of the stable Lagrangian category and conjectures
proof_stages.summary: The main construction is announced as a stable infinity-category Lag_lambda(M) with exact branes as objects, Lagrangian cobordisms as morphisms, a natural functor to the Fukaya category, and conjectural equivalence after localization together with locality.

proof_stages.label: Three motivations for Lagrangian-cobordism morphisms
proof_stages.summary: The morphism definition is motivated by the analogy between singular chains and Morse/Floer complexes, by noncharacteristic propagation as invariance of measurements under allowable motions, and by Pontryagin-Thom/topological-field-theoretic cobordism methods.

proof_stages.label: Naive cobordism category and its infinity enhancement
proof_stages.summary: A first version takes morphisms from L0 to L1 to be exact Lagrangian branes in M x T*R with time-collared ends, composes them by concatenation up to time reparametrization, and promotes the construction to an infinity-category by allowing families over simplices.

proof_stages.label: Noncharacteristic condition forced by collapse of the naive category
proof_stages.summary: The naive category has the empty brane as a zero object and in fact collapses, so the support lambda is introduced by restricting to lambda-noncharacteristic cobordisms, requiring a small positive dt-translate to avoid lambda near the zero section.

proof_stages.label: Stabilization and tautological cones
proof_stages.summary: The actual stable category is obtained by stabilizing the noncharacteristic cobordism categories under products with R^k, and in this stabilized setting cones are given tautologically by reinterpreting the cobordism time direction as a spatial cotangent direction and adding tails.

proof_stages.label: Functor to the Fukaya category and nontriviality
proof_stages.summary: A cobordism P is sent to a Fukaya morphism by extending the end Lagrangians and counting teardrop holomorphic disks, giving a well-defined functor that is identity on objects and showing the construction is not merely zero.

examples.name: Constructible sheaf on R and moving an interval
examples.note: A constructible sheaf on the line and a moving open interval show how singular support controls when measurements are invariant, leading to the universal noncharacteristic-propagation principle for the proposed category.
examples.engagement_reason: he works through a constructible sheaf on R with an interval U, passes to singular support and L_U in T*R, and checks when moving U preserves sections
